Name: -1_usc_angeles_social_president
Representative_Docs: ['USC cutting 259 jobs as it tackles rising $230 million deficit The University of Southern California began cutting 259 jobs on Friday as it tackles a rising operating deficit of more than $230 million Since July 9, USC has laid off — or is in the process of terminating — 975 people throughout the university system’s nearly 24,600 faculty and staff. That tally doesn’t include 9,185 student workers. The university is “reducing and reorganizing its workforce due to cutbacks in funding and necessary budget reductions,” wrote Melissa Gerdes-Leonard, associate vice president of client services with USC’s human resources department, in an Oct. 27 letter filed with the state’s Employment Development Department. Also see: USC won’t sign Trump compact on federal funding The latest round of layoffs are happening at USC’s University Park, a leafy 229-acre main campus for the institution’s academic schools and residential buildings near Exposition Park in downtown Los Angeles. After the cuts are completed Dec. 31, USC will have shed 616 people on its main campus, or two-thirds of the workforce laid off to date. Some of the positions cut include a marketing director, procurement specialist, research administrator, office managers, library supervisor, event', 'USC cutting 259 staff members to tackle rising $230 million deficit The University of Southern California began cutting 259 jobs on Friday as it tackles a rising operating deficit of more than $230 million Since July 9, USC has laid off — or is in the process of terminating — 975 people throughout the university system’s nearly 24,600 faculty and staff. That tally doesn’t include 9,185 student workers. The university is “reducing and reorganizing its workforce due to cutbacks in funding and necessary budget reductions,” wrote Melissa Gerdes-Leonard, associate vice president of client services with USC’s human resources department, in an Oct. 27 letter filed with the state’s Employment Development Department. Also see: USC won’t sign Trump compact on federal funding The latest round of layoffs are happening at USC’s University Park, a leafy 229-acre main campus for the institution’s academic schools and residential buildings near Exposition Park in downtown Los Angeles. After the cuts are completed Dec. 31, USC will have shed 616 people on its main campus, or two-thirds of the workforce laid off to date. Name: 2_united_arsenal_villa_chelsea
Representative_Docs: ['Doku, Bobb, Stones, Rodri, Kovacic latest: Man City injury list for West Ham clash By Oliver Thomas | 18 Dec 2025 08:05, Last updated: 18 Dec 2025 08:09 Manchester City will be looking to win their seventh game in a row in all competitions when they welcome West Ham United to the Etihad Stadium for their latest Premier League fixture on Saturday afternoon. The Citizens are in high spirits after securing their spot in the semi-finals of the EFL Cup with a 2-0 home victory over Brentford on Wednesday night, Prior to that triumph, Pep Guardiola’s men beat Crystal Palace by a 3-0 scoreline in the Premier League to climb up to second place in the table and move to within two points of leaders Arsenal. Here, Sports Mole rounds up Man City’s latest injury and suspension news ahead of the contest against the Hammers. Status: Major doubt Type of injury: Hamstring Possible return date: Unknown Oscar Bobb was forced off with an apparent hamstring injury in the EFL Cup win over Brentford and it remains to be seen whether he will be facing a period on the sidelines. Status: Out Type of injury: Leg Possible return date: January 1 (vs. Sunderland) Jeremy Doku has missed Man City’s last two games with a leg injury and Guardiola expects the winger to be sidelined for the next two', "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Sport | Football Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Pressure was piling on Arne Slot after the Reds slumped to ninth in the table, but even without a number of key players, the champions were able to sweep Villa aside to move back up to third. They welcome Real Madrid - Trent Alexander-Arnold, Xabi Alonso, et al - to Anfield in midweek, before they take on Manchester City in a mouth-watering encounter at the weekend. But will they have any of their star names back from injury? Ahead of a big few weeks, here’s all the latest Liverpool injury news and return dates... Alexander Isak has endured a tough start to life on Merseyside. Subbed at half-time in Liverpool’s 5-1 win over Frankfurt in the Champions League, Slot later revealed the £125million summer signing had suffered a minor groin injury. “Alexander had to go off at half-time because he felt his groin a little bit. That's a pity,” said Slot. “I've said many times it is such a difficult balance to find with a player that missed three months.” Isak was not in the squad in the 3-2 loss at Brentford, and he also missed out on the Caraba", "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Name: 3_passed away_jane_grandmother_died
Representative_Docs: ['Tosca Theresa Pardini Birth: 1931 Death: 2025 Tosca Theresa Pardini, beloved mother, Nonna, great grandmother, and cherished friend, passed away peacefully on Sunday, November 16, surrounded by the love of her family. On a dark, cloudy day, as the Frank Sinatra song “Fly Me to the Moon” played softly, a single ray of sunshine broke through and gently touched her beautiful face as she took her final breath with her family by her side. She was born at home in San Francisco on May 7, 1931, and she carried the spirit of the city with her throughout her life. Tosca later made her home in Ross, California, where she built a life centered on family, tradition, and enduring love. Tosca met the love of her life, Sergio Pardini, and they were married on February 17, 1952, at Saints Peter and Paul Church in North Beach. For sixty one adventure filled years they were inseparable and shared a deep devotion that guided their lives together. Known for her warmth, elegance, and quiet strength, Tosca created a home where everyone felt welcome. She was deeply proud of her Italian heritage and the traditions she lovingly passed down. Her kindness and grace touched all who knew her. Tosca loved traveling the world with Ser', 'Jane Marie Solaro Birth: 1934 Death: 2026 Jane passed away peacefully on January 4, 2026 at the age of 91. Jane was born in Pacific Grove, CA on December 14, 1934 and lived in San Pedro her entire life. She is preceded in death by her loving husband John Solaro and her parents Matthew Bodul & Ann (Berkovich) Bodul. Jane enjoyed the last four years at the Canterbury in Rancho Palos Verdes with her loving friends and caretakers. She is survived by her loving children, Karen Solaro Durber (Martin), Denise Solaro and John Solaro (Lynn). She was a loving grandmother to Anthony Durber (Christina), Michelle Durber Dilly (Ian), Mark Durber, Nikki Solaro, Ali Solaro Sandoval (Dustin), Jack Solaro and Max Solaro and her great grandchildren Brooklynn and Logan Dilly She is also survived by her siblings Bob Bodul (Marylou), Sharon Bodul Werth (Fred) and Matthew Bodul (Angie) as well as several nieces and nephews. She will be deeply missed and remembered by all. Graveside burial at Green Hills Cemetery on Friday, January 23, 2026 @ 2 pm. Celebration of Life to follow. Honor with Flowers In memory of Jane Marie Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Jane Marie', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 4_surgery_hospitals_nhs_medical
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques. Name: 5_cricket_world cup_icc_zealand
Representative_Docs: ['Australia’s Hazlewood unlikely to be fit for half of World Cup, Cummins says Australian fast bowler Josh Hazlewood may not return to action until halfway through the T20 World Cup, injured teammate Pat Cummins said on Tuesday. Hazlewood, arguably the squad’s most important bowler, has struggled with hamstring and Achilles injuries and missed the entire five-Test Ashes series against England. Australia head coach Andrew McDonald said in mid-January Hazlewood was on track to be fit for the squad’s World Cup opener against Ireland on February 11, but Cummins’s update was less optimistic. “I caught up with Joshy a few days ago, and he’s working so hard. I really feel for Joshy. He’s had a few setbacks. [He’s] one of the hardest trainers,” Cummins told Australian broadcaster Nine Network. “He’s doing everything he can. I don’t think he will be there for the start of the tournament, but he’s trying his best to be there about halfway through.” With Mitchell Starc having retired from the format and Cummins sidelined with back problems, Australia head into the February 7 to March 8 World Cup undermanned and in poor form after a 111-run humiliation by Pakistan, which completed a 3-0 series whitewash. Cummins said players’ late arrival to Pakistan after the domestic B', 'In-form England must keep developing to have Six Nations chance, says George By Mitch Phillips England hooker and former captain Jamie George has welcomed the confidence swirling around the team going into the Six Nations but said they cannot expect to win the title by playing the same way as they did to build an 11-match winning streak last year. England last won the tournament in 2020, and their most recent Grand Slam was in 2016, both under Eddie Jones, but they go into their opening match at home to Wales on February 7 with more confidence that they can top the standings than for many years. After narrowly losing their championship opener to Ireland in Dublin almost a year ago, England have chalked up 11 successive wins, including a rare one against New Zealand, and coach Steve Borthwick has built considerable depth in his squad. “We welcome the expectation because it’s a compliment for what we’ve done and what we’ve achieved over a short period of time, but it also keeps us on our toes because we know we can’t be complacent and can’t rely on previous performances,” George said at the Six Nations launch in Edinburgh on Monday. If we’re the same team as we were for the last 11 games, it will not be good enough, so our focus over the next two weeks is to', 'In-form England must keep developing to have Six Nations chance, says George By Mitch Phillips England hooker and former captain Jamie George has welcomed the confidence swirling around the team going into the Six Nations but said they cannot expect to win the title by playing the same way as they did to build an 11-match winning streak last year. Name: 6_passed away_jim_funeral_grandfather
Representative_Docs: ['John Kevin King Birth: 1956 Death: 2025 John Kevin King of La Habra Heights, California, passed away peacefully on November 5, 2025. He was born in Tulsa, Oklahoma, and was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, and friend. John earned his masters degree in civil engineering from Oklahoma State University and built a respected career marked by professionalism, responsibility, and a steady leadership style. He was known for his integrity, calm presence, and thoughtful approach to his work. He was an avid golfer and a longtime member of Hacienda Golf Club, where he also served as a board member. He valued the friendships, camaraderie, and community he found there. John was preceded in death by his parents, Charlie King and Juanita May King. He is survived by his wife, Allis King, his daughters Megan Branagh and her husband John, and Whitney Dugan and her husband Adam, and his grandchildren Grady, Henry, Wesley, and Georgie Branagh, and Oliver and Charlie Dugan. He loved his wife deeply and was known by many as a genuine and easygoing friend. A Celebration of Life will be held on November 23, 2025, in La Habra Heights, California. The family invites friends and loved ones to join in honoring his mem', 'John Hall Birth: 1933 Death: 2026 John was born October 7, 1933 in Chico, California to John and Alice Hall. He was raised on the family ranch in Cana and then served in the United States Army. After his service in the army he married and spent time in Corning where he worked for an olive plant. He then moved with his family to Chico and spent time working in the rice industry and at Jessup Door Lumber. He also spent some time working for Wieck Automotive. John loved playing his guitar and singing country western music. He enjoyed family, having fun and playing pranks on people. He is predeceased by his parents, 3 siblings and a son Dan Janes. John is survived by his sister Helen Burch, children Linda Field (Steve), Bill Janes (Lynn), Lee Hall (Jackie), Judy Wieck, and numerous grandchildren and great grandchildren which he loved to brag about. John passed away peacefully January 8, 2026 in Sparks, Nevada. A Celebration of Life will be held at 11:00am Saturday, January 31, 2026 at Newton-Bracewell Chico Funeral Home. We will be celebrating our love for John by wearing his favorite clothing style of western attire to his celebration. Please feel free to join us by wearing your western attire.', 'James Rogers Birth: 1938 Death: 2025 James R. Rogers, known as “Jim” to his family and friends, was born in San Francisco on April 21, 1938. He passed away peacefully at his home in Los Altos on December 2, 2025 at the age of 87. Jim was married to the love of his life, Patricia Hughes Rogers, for over 61 years before her passing on December 17, 2024. Together, Jim and Pat were a dynamic duo who enjoyed all of the great moments in life together-from creating a loving family, to building houses and businesses, to exploring the world together. They are survived by their daughter Kathleen (Mark), son Peter (Julie), and four grandchildren (Samantha, Christopher, Jeremiah, and Nathan). Growing up, Jim attended Saint Ignatius High School in San Francisco and graduated from San Jose State with a degree in Civil Engineering. He spent his working years on the Peninsula developing real estate. Jim had boundless energy and a keen interest in the world around him. He was a charming, friendly, positive person who was quick to make friends wherever he went, many for life. He had a mischievous sense of humor and ability to make adventures with him fun for all involved, whether embarking on world travels, enjo']

Name: 8_family_family members_gifts_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Representative_Docs: ["How To Master The Wall-To-Wall Carpet Trend Without Looking Dated Wall-to-wall carpeting has had a rough run in the past couple decades. For years, it was a staple of American home design, lining everywhere from family room floors to basement recreation rooms. But over the past decades, the look has gone more out of style, with people leaning towards fresh, minimalist spaces with flooring options like hardwood, luxury vinyl, or laminate. Many homeowners can understand the potential benefits of carpeting, but often fear the look is just too dated for their modern spaces. But wall-to-wall carpeting isn't a quitter. In recent years, it's crept back into the scene for a comeback. Now, it's one of the carpet floor trends that is having a moment, particularly in bedrooms. It's not hard to see why homeowners like the idea of a carpeted bedroom floor — carpeting can be budget-friendly, provides sound absorption, and can make a space feel more warm and cozy. Of course, to many homeowners, comfort matters just as much as aesthetics in bedroom areas. To get that softer feel while avoiding making the space look too dated, designers suggest opting for loop pile, specifically berber carpeting. Name: 12_museum_egypt_egyptian_world largest
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 13_africa_school students_south africa_tourism
Representative_Docs: ['Minnesota high schoolers prepare for once-in-lifetime learning trip to Africa ASHBY, Minn. — There are field trips and there are field trips. Students at Ashby High School are busy raising money and getting ready to pack their bags for a two-week learning experience in South Africa. ADVERTISEMENT When it is below zero, the main street in Ashby, Minnesota, is quiet. Fewer than 500 people call this Grant County town home, but lately the town is busy rallying around a group of high school students. In the basement of Peace Grue Lutheran in the middle of town, five high school students join veteran church quilters in tying quilts that the students will take on their trip to South Africa. "There is a reason the church ladies do this and not the guys," joked Kevin Fick, the girls\' weightlifting coach and organizer of the trip, of tying quilts the students will take with them as gifts. Five girls, along with Fick and four adult chaperones, will make the trip to Eastern Cape, South Africa, from Feb. 28 through March 10. Not as tourists, but to learn about conservation on another continent. "Not a lot of kids get to do this," said student Hadley Hokanson, student. They will work a sheep ranch, fish for sharks, harvest their own food, camp in the bushes and more. Name: 15_immigration_britain_immigrants_diversity
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 17_santa_christmas_holiday season_festive
Representative_Docs: ['Celebrating Christmas in and around Larnaca The city, the suburbs and the villages of Larnaca all get into the festive mood this season as dozens of Christmas events pop up. All around the Larnaca region, markets, workshops and performances will share magical moments for visitors. The SanTa Lefkara Christmas Village is on until January 6 with artists’ stalls, entertainment, craft workshops, children’s activities and concerts taking over the village centre. As one of the deputy ministry of tourism’s eight Christmas Villages, Lefkara is dressed in full holiday glam with glimmering string lights, Christmas trees and a rich, weekly entertainment programme. On December’s first weekend coming up, Larnaca region’s biggest fair returns. The Traditional Christmas Market makes its anticipated annual comeback at Forest Beach, Dekeleia Road, bringing over 200 stalls selling a wide range of food, craft and handmade items from handbags to sweets, candles, baked goods and hundreds of other unique items. The market will include Santa visits, live radio links, hot drinks and food stands. The Livadia Christmas Programme will also bring holiday cheer on December 6, 14, 20 and 28 as the community square fills with music and dance beats, the fam', 'Want an ‘ezbacke uvin’ or ‘mot cantrl’ for Christmas? Write to Santa The time has come again for both the young and old to send in their letters to Santa. This has become one of my favorite Christmas traditions. The letters to Santa we publish each December are often heartwarming and occasionally heartbreaking. They are a reminder of what makes Christmas magical. They are an amusing break from the sometimes jarring news we must print in the paper. And, hey, they might even be beneficial to your brain’s elasticity. It takes some creativity on the reader’s part to figure out just what the little kiddies are asking for, since we publish the letters verbatim. Try to decipher these phrases from previous Santa letters: I’m looking forward to the, umm, creative writing in this year’s batch of letters. We will publish Santa letters from children and adults in north central Idaho and southeastern Washington. The letters can be just a few words if you have a short wish list, but please try to write no more than 300 words. Be sure to include the author’s name, age and town of residence. The letters can be emailed to city@lmtribune.com, sent in the mail to Letters to Santa, Lewiston Tribune, 505 Capital St., Lewiston, ID 83501, or dropped off at our office. Representative_Docs: ["Prince Harry's visa row back in spotlight as Andrew asked to testify in US Prince Harry’s visa row has been brought back into the spotlight as a result of the latest news that Andrew Mountbatten Windsor has been asked to appear before the US Congress for questioning over his links to convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. On Thursday evening, it was revealed that Andrew, who has recently been stripped of all his royal titles as a result of scandals surrounding his past friendship with Epstein, has now been asked to give evidence in the US. Andrew has always denied any allegations made against him. While this has led to mixed public reaction, one public figure has said that if this is the case for Andrew, Prince Harry should also be asked to testify before Congress in the US about his visa. This is because over recent years, think tank group The Heritage Foundation have repeatedly asked for Harry’s US visa to be made public to see if he lied about his past drug use. This is because Harry has lived in America since 2020, but revealed in his 2023 memoir that he took drugs as a teenager. Representative_Docs: ['How much will DWP benefits and pensions rise following the budget? Benefits Budget 2025 Child Benefit Department for Work and Pensions DWP Pensions PIP Rachel Reeves Universal Credit National government Personal Finance DWP benefits including Universal Credit, Child Benefit, PIP, Carer\'s Allowance, and the State Pension are to increase in April 2026 after Rachel Reeves announced higher payments in her budget speech today. Anyone who claims DWP benefits should be in line for an increase in the next financial year, as the chancellor laid out plans to help pensioners, disabled people, parents, low earners, carers and unemployed people keep up with rising costs. Addressing MPs, Ms Reeves said: "The broken welfare system that we inherited wrote off millions of people as too sick to work and we will reform that system so that it is a system that does not count the cost of failure, but one that protects people who cannot work and empowers those who can. "We have brought back face-to-face assessments for disability benefits. Name: 22_baseball_major league_pitches_garza
Representative_Docs: ['AP Sports SummaryBrief at 3:08 p.m. EST MLB pitchers Emmanuel Clase and Luis Ortiz charged with taking bribes to rig pitches for bettors NEW YORK (AP) — Two Major League Baseball pitchers have been indicted on charges they took bribes to give sports bettors advance notice of the types of pitches they’d throw and intentionally tossed balls instead of strikes to ensure successful bets. Emmanuel Clase and Luis Ortiz both pitched for the Cleveland Guardians. They have been on non-disciplinary paid leave since July while Major League Baseball investigated what it said was unusually high in-game betting activity when they pitched. A lawyer for Ortiz declined to comment when contacted by The Associated Press and a lawyer for Clase did not immediately return messages seeking comment. Paul Tagliabue, NFL commissioner of 17 years who led an era of riches and expansion, dies at 84 NEW YORK (AP) — Paul Tagliabue, who helped bring labor peace and riches to the NFL during his 17 years as commissioner but was criticized for not taking stronger action on concussions, died Sunday from heart failure at 84 years old. NFL spokesman Brian McCarthy said Tagliabue’s family informed the league of his death in Chevy Chase, Maryland. Tagliabue, w', "Braves' Schwellenbach undergoes surgery; procedure on tap for Waldrep Hurston Waldrep FILE - Atlanta Braves' Spencer Schwellenbach pitches to a San Francisco Giants batter during the first inning of a baseball game, June 6, 2025, in San Francisco. (AP Photo/Godofredo A. Vásquez, File) NORTH PORT, Fla. — Atlanta Braves right-handed starter Spencer Schwellenbach had surgery Wednesday to clean up “loose bodies” in his elbow, and rotation candidate Hurston Waldrep is scheduled to undergo a similar procedure next week. While the Braves didn’t reveal a timeline for when the pitchers could be available, manager Walt Weiss told reporters Wednesday that he is hopeful that both will be able to pitch this season. Schwellenbach was placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 10, at the start of spring training, because of inflammation in his right elbow. The 25-year-old was 7-4 with a 3.08 ERA in 17 starts last year before missing the final three months of the season because of a broken right elbow. Waldrep, the Braves’ first-round pick out of Florida in the 2023 amateur draft, experienced discomfort after throwing batting practice over the weekend. An MRI showed no ligament damage for the pitcher who turns 24 on March 1. Name: 23_inch display_monitors_tvs_monitor
Representative_Docs: ['Luckfox 16-inch 4K 120Hz portable touch monitor offers 100% sRGB coverage The segment of portable monitors continues to grow, with many models available on Amazon at relatively affordable prices, typically between $100 and $150. These screens can be useful for productivity on business trips or while traveling. But portable monitors that combine a high resolution with a fast refresh rate remain uncommon in the mid-range segment. Luckfox wants to address this gap with its new 16" 4K Touch Monitor. The 16-inch display features a 3840 × 2400 (WQUXGA) IPS panel with 350 nits brightness and a 1200:1 contrast ratio. It uses optical bonding technology with 6H-hardness toughened glass and a matte finish to minimize ambient reflections. The 16:10 aspect ratio provides more vertical workspace than the standard 16:9 format. It also supports a 120Hz refresh rate, which is an upgrade over the common 60Hz panels found in many portable monitors, such as the Auzai ME16Z01 or the Arzopa A1M; response times aren\'t disclosed, though. As for connectivity, the product integrates two USB Type-C ports and a Mini HDMI input. Both USB-C ports support audio, video, and power delivery, meaning that only a single USB-C cable is required when connected to a compatible smartphone, tab', 'Samsung OLED 48S95H: New 48-inch and 165 Hz OLED TV revealed ahead of schedule with HDR10 Plus Advanced support Samsung has already teased plenty of devices on the eve of CES 2026. However, the trade show has decided to lift the lid on one of Samsung\'s forthcoming OLED Smart TVs. For context, Samsung listed numerous unreleased Smart TVs on its Parts Database over a month ago. Of those, CES has announced that the S95H is a 2026 Honoree in its Gaming & eSports category. Specifically, the trade show has signalled out a 48-inch variant that was missing from November\'s leak. Incidentally, last year\'s S95F only went as small as 55-inches (curr. $1,997 on Amazon). According to the listing, the S95H offers a 165 Hz refresh rate with VRR technology supported. Assuming a 4K resolution from the S95F series and the usage of HDMI 2.1 ports, a 165 Hz refresh rate would require the usage of Display Stream Compression (DSC) to output at its peak resolution and refresh rate simultaneously. Moreover, the new 48-inch Smart TV will support Samsung\'s new HDR10 Plus Advanced format. Pricing and availability are unknown for now; the full description provided by Samsung and CES is as follows: The Samsung OLED (S95H) is the world\'s most advanced 48" OLED TV for high-end gaming. The display is intended to deliver immersive experiences on both sides simultaneously, with built-in audio and dynamic lighting effects. Name: 24_federal reserve_florence_meeting_italy
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated. Representative_Docs: ["UK Championship snooker referee is banned from officiating one player at the tournament A top snooker referee is barred from taking charge of one player's matches at the UK Championship - because she is married to him. Belarus-born Tatiana Woollaston, 38, will be one of the officials on duty at the first Triple Crown event of the season, which begins on Saturday. Another Woollaston will also be involved at the York Barbican, her husband Ben, who booked his place in the 32-player showpiece by coming through qualifying earlier this week. He is now looking forward to a first-round clash with three-time UK champion John Higgins on Saturday night. However, there is no chance of Tatiana taking charge of the match. She told the Mirror ahead of last year's UK Championship that they try to keep their respective snooker careers as separate as possible. You', 'Strictly Come Dancing star breaks silence after concern fifth contestant would quit Strictly Come Dancing\'s Alex Kingston is poised to carry on with her dance adventure, putting to rest concerns she might have to withdraw due to injury. The 62-year-old actress had triggered concerns about a potential exit after her daughter disclosed a rib injury on social media, stating that her mother had endured the discomfort to participate in Saturday\'s programme. The Doctor Who legend, who has been dazzling audiences on the dance floor alongside partner Johannes Radebe, was swift to comfort her supporters that injuries were simply "part and parcel" of the Strictly experience, and she had absolutely no plans to retire her dancing shoes. Representative_Docs: ['Mid-season WMA waterfowl quota hunt app period to be held Oct. 22-Nov. 12 TWRA News Tennessee Wildlife Resources Agency (TWRA) announced that the application period for the 2025 Mid-Season Wildlife Management Area (WMA) Waterfowl Quota Hunt will be open from Wednesday, Oct. 22 through Wednesday, Nov. 12. Applications can be submitted online at gooutdoorstennessee.com, through the TWRA mobile app, at TWRA regional offices, or at any TWRA license agent. Mailed applications will not be accepted, and applicants must be at least 16 years old. Detailed application instructions, WMA locations, and general waterfowl quota hunt information are available on the TWRA Quota Hunts webpage. The deadline to submit applications is Wednesday, Nov. 12 at 11:59 p.m. (CST). A person may only submit one application, which can include up to 48 unique hunt choices; hunt choices may not be repeated. Qualifying parties must have at least four members, with a maximum of eight. Successful applicants will receive an email with instructions to log in and must claim their permit by Monday, Nov. 24 at 11:59 p.m. CST. Each party member must claim their own permit. Permits will be available to print after they have been claimed.
